text.title: csharp-background-services Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-background-services as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Task.Delay loop used as a timer substitute.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Task.Delay does not integrate with the host lifetime cleanly; cancellation after Delay starts requires careful token forwarding. PeriodicTimer is the idiomatic replacement in .NET 6+.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates a silent memory leak under load. Always use Channel.CreateBounded with an explicit capacity and FullMode.

failure-mode.description: DbContext or other scoped service injected directly into BackgroundService constructor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: BackgroundService is a singleton; scoped services throw at runtime on first use. Resolve per-item inside ExecuteAsync via CreateScope.

failure-mode.description: ExecuteAsync without try/catch around the loop body.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One transient exception terminates the consumer loop; subsequent items are never processed.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Service compiles and injects fine but never starts — silent runtime bug.

failure-mode.description: Multi-replica deploy running BackgroundService on every pod.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leads to double-processing. Gate with leader election or migrate to Hangfire/MassTransit.
